raw_output:
Imagine you're blowing bubbles with your breath—those bubbles float up because air pushes them! Airplanes fly the same way.  

Their wings are shaped like a smile (curved on top, flatter on the bottom). When the plane moves fast through the air, the air rushes *over* the top of the wing. This makes the air move faster and push the wing *up*—just like how blowing on a leaf makes it float!  

The engine helps the plane move fast enough so the wings can push the air up and keep the plane flying. It’s like the wings are tiny air balloons that *want* to go up, but they need the plane to zoom through the air first!  

So next time you see a plane in the sky, remember: it’s all about the wings pushing air up—just like your breath can make bubbles float! 🌬️✈️
